text: Here we reveal, through a detailed investigation using combination of GCDFT, global optimization techniques, quasi-kinetic Monte Carlo (MC) simulations, and classical molecular dynamics and free energy calculations, the dynamic properties of such restructuring and their relation to reactivity toward $CO_2$ ER. We investigated how Cu surfaces structurally evolve as a function of applied potential in solutions containing various concentrations of CO and H 2, and revealed the underlying causes of Cu ER selectivity. Our simulations predicted that the chemical structural motif on the Cu surface, i.e., atomic vacancies and adatoms, are strongly dependent on potential, pH, and the presence of $CO_2$, and indicated that dynamic restructuring of copper surfaces has a complex nature. The regimes of different structural motifs at the Cu electrode in solutions containing mixtures of $CO_2$ and $H_2$ were obtained, including the effects of various species present in solutions on structural changes of Cu surfaces and demonstrated that a quantitative prediction of the behavior of catalysts during electrochemical reactions will require information about both thermodynamic structural changes of the catalyst during reactions and dynamic structural changes of the catalyst during reactions. Overall, our work revealed how the structural behavior of copper catalysts affects their reactivity during the $CO_2$ ER.
